title: Past Perfect Continuous
definition: The Past Perfect Continuous tense emphasizes the duration of an action that was ongoing before another past action or time.

structure.positive: I had been working there for five years before I resigned.
structure.negative: I had not (hadn't) been working there long before I resigned.
structure.question: Had you been working there long before you resigned?
structure.whQuestion: How long had you been working there before you resigned?

usage: Use Past Perfect Continuous to emphasize how long an action had been happening before another past event (She had been studying for three hours before the exam started), and to explain the cause of a past situation (He was tired because he had been running).
examples: She had been studying for three hours before the exam started.
He was tired because he had been running.
They had been waiting for an hour before the bus arrived.
I had been living there for ten years before I moved.
Had you been working all night before the meeting?
It had been raining for days before the flood happened.
How long had she been waiting before you called?

dailyConversation.speaker: B
dailyConversation.line: He had been working overtime all week.

dailyConversation.speaker: A
dailyConversation.line: How long had you been waiting before the bus came?

dailyConversation.speaker: B
dailyConversation.line: I had been waiting for almost an hour.

commonMistakes.wrong: I had been know her for years.
commonMistakes.right: I had known her for years.
commonMistakes.why: State verbs like 'know' don't take continuous forms; use Past Perfect simple instead.

commonMistakes.wrong: She had been study before the test.
commonMistakes.right: She had been studying before the test.
commonMistakes.why: Use V-ing after 'had been', not the base verb.

commonMistakes.wrong: He was working there since 2010 before he left.
commonMistakes.right: He had been working there since 2010 before he left.
commonMistakes.why: Use Past Perfect Continuous, not Past Continuous, for duration before another past event.

quiz.question: Choose the correct sentence:
options: I had been waiting for an hour.
I had waiting for an hour.
I have been waiting for an hour before.
I was been waiting for an hour.
quiz.answer: I had been waiting for an hour.

quiz.question: She ___ (study) for hours before the test began.
quiz.explanation: Emphasizes duration before a past event.

quiz.question: Which situation best fits Past Perfect Continuous?
options: Duration of an action before another past event
quiz.answer: Duration of an action before another past event
quiz.explanation: This tense highlights how long something had been happening before a past point.
